subject: Project Management
title: The Kanban Card
description: A 60-second lesson on The Kanban Card.

hook.text: The word 'kanban' means 'visual signal' in Japanese. It started as a physical card.

buildup.text: When a station used a part, it sent a kanban card upstream: 'I need more of this.'

discovery.text: No card, no production. The card was a physical limit on how much work was in progress.

twist.text: This simple card system replaced complex scheduling software. Less technology, more discipline.

climax.text: A small piece of cardboard became one of manufacturing's most powerful management tools.

punchline.text: The most powerful signal was the simplest one.

quiz.question: What does the word 'kanban' mean in Japanese?
